subject: Mathematics
chapter_name: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Solid Shapes)
overall_learning_objectives: Define Faces, Edges, and Vertices (F, E, V)
Identify and draw Nets for various 3D solids
Learn to make Oblique Sketches of solids
Learn to make Isometric Sketches on dot paper
Relate 2D nets to their final 3D forms

lesson_plan.title: Faces, Edges, and Vertices
topics_covered: Counting F, E, and V in Polyhedrons
teaching_strategy: [Image showing faces, edges, and vertices of a cuboid]
student_activities: Table completion: Shape name | No. of Faces | No. of Edges | No. of Vertices
Build a triangular pyramid using toothpicks and marshmallows

lesson_plan.title: Nets and Sketches
topics_covered: Drawing cubes and cuboids on squared and dot paper
student_activities: Draw three different possible nets for a cube
Practice drawing a 2x2x2 cube on isometric dot paper
Match the net to its solid in a worksheet

teacher_tip: When teaching nets, remind students that a net is a 'jacket' for the solid—if the jacket doesn't close or has overlapping parts, it’s not a valid net!
